cover.hook: Two Ring of Fire corridor projects are simultaneously under construction — for the first time.
cover.sub: Plus OpenAI's first custom chip, Thunder Bay recruiting from Toronto, trades training going local, and 13 fires across the Northwest.

reelSub: AI is getting cheaper and the Ring of Fire is building on two fronts.
storySub: What this week's moves mean for a Northwestern Ontario business.

items.title: Geraldton's Ring of Fire gateway gets rebuilt
items.body: $81.3M reconstruction of Geraldton's main corridor — awarded to a First Nations firm — prepares the Ring of Fire gateway. Ring of Fire corridor infrastructure now under active construction on multiple fronts.
items.tease: Next: AI is about to get cheaper.
items.reelLine: $81.3M Ring of Fire road rebuild — the corridor is widening.

items.title: OpenAI built its first chip in 9 months — inference costs are heading down
items.body: The Jalapeño processor, built with Broadcom, is designed for running AI models cheaper and faster. Custom silicon from model providers means lower per-query costs ahead.
items.tease: Next: Thunder Bay is recruiting your next hire from southern Ontario.
items.reelLine: OpenAI built its own chip in 9 months. AI inference is getting cheaper.

items.title: Thunder Bay recruiting tradespeople and professionals from across Canada
items.body: The CEDC 'Work Here' campaign runs nationally through September with GO Transit ads and workinthunderbay.ca. Regional employers benefit from the brand-awareness lift at no direct cost.
items.tease: Next: trades apprentices can now train locally in Thunder Bay.
items.reelLine: Thunder Bay is running a national campaign to fill your skilled-trades job listings.

items.title: Confederation College approved to train General Machinists in Thunder Bay
items.body: Ontario approved the 429A machinist apprenticeship at Confederation College on June 26. Apprentices in mining, manufacturing, and equipment repair no longer need to travel south.
items.tease: Next: 13 fires burning across the Northwest.
items.reelLine: Confederation College can now train machinists — a win for NWO trades retention.

items.title: Thirteen active wildfires in the Northwest — elevated risk for outdoor operations
items.body: As of June 25, thirteen wildfires were active in the Northwest Region, four uncontrolled, with fire danger high to extreme. Forestry, construction, and outdoor operations should monitor the provincial fire portal.
items.reelLine: 13 active fires across the Northwest — know the risk if you operate outdoors.

summary.title: Infrastructure builds, AI costs fall, and the labour market gets regional support
summary.body: This week's NWO picture: Ring of Fire corridor infrastructure enters active construction on multiple fronts, AI economics are shifting as model providers move to custom silicon, and regional institutions are doing the talent-attraction work individual employers cannot.

captions.carousel: Two Ring of Fire corridor projects are simultaneously under construction. OpenAI unveiled its first custom inference chip — and per-query AI costs are heading down. Thunder Bay is running a national campaign to attract tradespeople from across Canada. Confederation College can now train General Machinists locally. Five developments from this week in the Northwest — full breakdown in the Signal, link in bio. #ThunderBay #NorthwesternOntario #NorthernOntario #RingOfFire #AIbusiness #NWOntario
captions.reel: Ring of Fire building on two fronts. OpenAI's first custom chip. Thunder Bay recruiting nationally. Five moves for the Northwest this week. thunderbayai.com #NorthernOntario #ThunderBay #RingOfFire #AIbusiness
